## xUnit.net v3

xUnit.net is a developer testing framework, built to support Test Driven Development, with a design goal of extreme simplicity and alignment with framework features. Supports .NET Framework 4.7.2 or later and .NET 8 or later.

Installing this package installs xunit.v3.mtp-v1.

## About xUnit.net

xUnit.net is a free, open source, community-focused unit testing tool for C#, F#, and Visual Basic. xUnit.net v3 supports .NET 8.0 or later, and .NET Framework 4.7.2 or later.

xUnit.net works with the [.NET SDK](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download) command line tools, [Visual Studio](https://visualstudio.microsoft.com/), [Visual Studio Code](https://code.visualstudio.com/), [JetBrains Rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/), [NCrunch](https://www.ncrunch.net/), and any development environment compatible with [Microsoft Testing Platform](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/microsoft-testing-platform-intro) or [VSTest](https://github.com/microsoft/vstest).

xUnit.net is part of the [.NET Foundation](https://www.dotnetfoundation.org/) and operates under their [code of conduct](https://www.dotnetfoundation.org/code-of-conduct). The latest stable version of `xunit.v3` is `3.2.2`. Now let me do the migration.

Key changes needed:

1. **Directory.Packages.props**: 
   - Replace `xunit` v2.9.3 with `xunit.v3` v3.2.2
   - Remove `xunit.runner.visualstudio` (the runner is built into xunit.v3 via MTP)
   - Remove `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` (not needed with MTP)

2. **TestProject.csproj**:
   - Replace `<PackageReference Include="xunit" />` with `<PackageReference Include="xunit.v3" />`
   - Remove the runner and SDK references
   - Add `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` since xUnit v3 test projects are executables

3. **BasicTests.cs**: The test code stays unchanged—`using Xunit;`, `[Fact]` attributes, and `Assert` calls all work the same way in v3.

4. **global.json**: I need to update the test runner configuration for .NET 8 since xUnit v3 uses Microsoft.Testing.Platform.  The `dotnet test` command should work with the MTP integration that comes with xunit.v3, but I need to verify the right approach for .NET 8 compatibility. I should remove the VSTest runner specification from global.json since xUnit v3 uses MTP instead, and then verify the changes work by building and running the tests with `dotnet test` or `dotnet run`.
